Very impressed with the ventilation in the #kubecon public areas; the CO2 is usually in the 450-500 range; 435 in the press room with 10 people in here, which is about the same as being outside. Hope the session and lunch rooms and especially the parties are like that
Just landed at SLC for Kubecon. Where’s everybody at?

Arrived in Amsterdam for #KubeCon! Looking forward to seeing folks starting at Maintainer Summit on Sunday; who else will be here this week? (Also, enjoy a picture of a yummy galette from Cocotte, a delightful crêperie worth visiting if you’re here!)

Really excited about my #kubecon talk - it's all about how to make your AI pipeline better with observability. But NOT about the model ... it's about the data flowing into it. 

Come check it out! exso.cloud/2026-03-kube...

The #PlatformEngineeringDay greatness at the #KubeCon #CloudNativeCon doesn't stop: now we got @hazelweakly.me talking about building extensible platforms!

Things usually go this way: Strong components → system → complexity → components → ... Is the cycle unavoidable?

I wish there were more "outsider guides" to technologies, that explained what it is for people who hear about it or are adjacent to it but don't need to use it. Like what is Kubernetes? What problems does it solve? What makes it a hard problem to solve? What are the legendary warts?

Sadly, I will not be at KubeCon this week. To show all my kubernaut friends just how much I miss you, I have a small gift.

exploding.k8s.io

Go easy on me, it's just a "free time" project.

Have fun!

recent_posts.text: But I don't wanna be put into kubernetes! That's doll cruelty! I was here first so I will continue to pollute the global namespace >:<

npm supply chain attacks targeting Kubernetes infra is the nightmare scenario DevSecOps people have been warning about for years. The Iran-linked wiper component makes it genuinely terrifying. Scan your lockfiles, people. 🔒

The new CanisterWorm is spreading via npm, hijacking dev accounts and targeting Kubernetes, with a wiper that triggers on Iran-based systems

Read: hackread.com/canisterworm...

#CyberSecurity #Malware #Kubernetes #Iran #CanisterWorm
recent_posts.embed_title: New CanisterWorm Targets Kubernetes Clusters, Deploys “Kamikaze” Wiper

recent_posts.text: this is actually the real reason kubernetes has namespaces

TeamPCP targets Kubernetes clusters by installing the CanisterWorm backdoor on non-Iranian systems and deploying a geopolitically targeted wiper on clusters configured for Iran. Exploits include SSH and Docker API. #TeamPCP #Iran #Kubernetes

recent_posts.text:
I know all the cool kids are at KubeCon EU this week, but _this_ cool kid is gonna be at SRECon in Seattle!  I'm giving a talk: "Stop Reading Changelogs: Safer Kubernetes Upgrades with Simulation" on Wednesday at 11:55-12:40.

Come find me and say hi!  Would love to chat.

#srecon

Ahoy, #KubeCon friends, if y'all want to catch up, let me know! I don't have a ticket so I plan to post up at cafés and bars nearby throughout the week...

It would be especially great to catch up with old friends from the other side of the Atlantic!
How to get to the best happy hour at KubeCon? Follow this guide

No GPU expertise required. Your data never leaves your network.

recent_posts.embed_title: Building Inference-as-a-Service on Kubernetes

recent_posts.text: Hristo Stoychev has put together a good primer to check out if you work with #Kubernetes in any production capacity. It also ties everything back to CKA/CKS exam prep, which makes it a solid reference if you plan to study for these. (4/4)

It covers projected tokens, workload identity, PSS enforcement modes, and network policy defaults in a way that is both deep and usable. This is a good start to gain a better understanding of how security with Kubernetes works. (3/4)

https://lckhd.eu/90tAwp

Kubernetes security is layered, nuanced, and easy to get wrong. The article below discusses the full access control stack. 🧵
Helm 4 uses the Extism WebAssembly plugin to add sandboxed security and isolation to Kubernetes deployments without replacing the container orchestration platform.
recent_posts.embed_title: Why WebAssembly won't replace Kubernetes but makes Helm more secure

How fast can you speedrun Mario? If you're at #Kubecon EU, come play against our fuzzer. Winner gets trash-talking rights.

It’s nearly the end of the day and I’ve only just got my “I’ve arrived at KubeCon” selfie! 

#Kubecon #Platformengineering #OpenSourceSecurityCon

The issue with mutating admission: it won't update the existing resources. But Kyverno now has a mutateExisting that help with that: kyverno.io/docs/policy-...

Also, just learnt about github.com/kyverno/chai..., going to check it out
recent_posts.embed_title: Mutate Rules

recent_posts.text: Love the migration from JMESPath to CEL-Native policies! Having more type safety is always welcome. Gotta play more with that, still using JMESPath-based rules with (deprecated) Kyverno-JSON for a couple projects.

FluxCD covers such cases by default with Server-Side Apply nativaly.

For ArgoCD: argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/stable/us...
recent_posts.embed_title: Sync Options - Argo CD - Declarative GitOps CD for Kubernetes

recent_posts.text:
"Doesn't mutating admission violates GitOps principles?"

Kyverno & Mutation is all about contextual reality. While GitOps helps with the desired baseline, we still need to inject dynamic, env-specific logic.

But how to prevent conflicts between the GitOps world and the post-mutation one?

I've been advocating at @payfiteng.bsky.social for us to move our controls from the tooling (OpenTofu lint rules, Helm meta-charts, etc.) to the platform itself (Kubernetes Admission Controllers, AWS Config / SCPs, whatever). Hope to get more insights into how to approach that here!

The great thing with the format of the co-located events for a few years not at #KubeCon #CloudNativeCon is : you got to move between the events! Now to the #KyvernoCon to listen to Rodrigo Fior Kuntzer from Miro about a topic that I love: policies to shift platform logic to the control plane!

Capacitor is a local-first Kubernetes client with a built-in webserver that provides a general-purpose UI for FluxCD, including resource discovery, Helm history, diffing, the Flux resource tree, and multi-cluster support that respects RBAC
